pi_items.source: Verified by reading task.json, setup_task.sh, and export_result.sh which enforce and read this exact output path.
Verified by reading export_result.sh which uses range(925, 956) and verifier.py which checks elevations starting from 925.
Verified by reading export_result.sh which uses range(925, 956) and verifier.py which checks elevations ending at 955 inclusive.
Verified by reading verifier.py which asserts a 5.0 percent tolerance check between the agents derived volumes and the ground truth.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires deriving a stage-storage curve for the Muncie HEC-RAS model, a real-world example representing the White River in Indiana. The expected output path is /home/ga/Documents/hec_ras_results/reach_storage_curve.csv for elevations 925 to 955 ft with a 5.0% tolerance. I discovered a fatal defect in the tasks evaluation logic: the export_result.sh script attempts to read a River Stations dataset from the HDF5 geometry file that does not exist in the HEC-RAS version 6.x schema (it should use the Attributes dataset instead). This throws a KeyError during ground truth generation, causing verifier.py to receive an empty ground truth dictionary and inevitably fail the agent regardless of their solutions correctness.
